But I can give you information about "Uragoga," specifically "Ptychopetalum olacoides". 

Ptychopetalum olacoides, also known as Uragoga or Unguay, is a plant that belongs to the family Olacaceae. It is a small tree or shrub native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, particularly in Brazil.

The health benefits of Uragoga are primarily related to its potential to improve cognitive function, memory, and sleep quality, as well as its possible antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. However, more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.

The bioactive constituents of Uragoga include alkaloids, glycosides, and other phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its potential medicinal properties.

The most common herbal preparations made with Uragoga include extracts, capsules, and teas, which are often marketed as natural remedies for cognitive support and sleep aid.

It is native to tropical regions and belongs to the order Gentianales. The genus Uragoga is a monotypic genus, meaning it consists of only one species, Uragoga dichotoma. The classification of Uragoga dichotoma is further supported by molecular and morphological studies. The exact classification of Uragoga dichotoma may vary slightly depending on the source and the most recent taxonomic revisions.
uses: Uragoga dichotoma is known for its traditional medicinal uses, particularly in West African countries. The plant is used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. In some regions, Uragoga dichotoma is also used as a treatment for stomach problems and as a remedy for snake bites. However, the effectiveness of these uses requires further scientific investigation to confirm its medicinal properties.
